howTo.name: How to use the word and character counter
howTo.description: Count words, characters and sentences in your text. Use tools to change letter case, remove duplicates or sort lines.

steps.name: Paste your text
steps.text: Paste or type text into the text field. The tool will automatically count words, characters, sentences, paragraphs, unique words and estimate reading time.

steps.name: Check the statistics
steps.text: In the left panel you will see 8 text metrics: words, characters with and without spaces, sentences, paragraphs, unique words, average word length and reading time.

steps.text: Click Copy text to transfer the modified text to your clipboard. Use Clear to start over.

contentBlocks.title: Free word and character counter online with text tools
contentBlocks.html:
This tool combines a word and character counter with a set of text formatting functions. Paste your text and you will see the word count, characters, sentences, paragraphs, unique words and estimated reading time.

Below the text field you will find a toolbar with 10 tools: letter case conversion (UPPERCASE, lowercase, Sentence case, Title Case), removing extra spaces, empty lines and duplicates, and alphabetical sorting. All analysis and processing happens locally in your browser.

contentBlocks.title: How to use the word and character counter
contentBlocks.description: Analyze and format text in three steps:

items.title: 1. Paste your text
items.description: Paste or type text into the field on the right. The tool will immediately count words, characters, sentences, paragraphs, unique words and estimate reading time.

items.title: 2. Check the statistics
items.description: In the left panel you will see 8 metrics: words, characters with and without spaces, sentences, paragraphs, unique words, average word length and reading time.

items.title: 3. Transform text
items.description: Below the text field you will find a toolbar with 10 tools: letter case conversion, removing spaces, empty lines, duplicates and sorting lines.

items.title: 4. Copy the result
items.description: Click Copy text to transfer the modified text to your clipboard. Use Clear to start over.

contentBlocks.title: 8 text metrics — what the counter measures
contentBlocks.description: The counter analyzes your text across eight indicators:

items.title: Words
items.description: Total word count. The primary indicator of text length, useful for content creation, articles and descriptions.

items.title: Characters (with spaces)
items.description: All characters including spaces. Useful when a platform has a character limit (e.g., Amazon listings, meta descriptions).

items.title: Characters (without spaces)
items.description: Only letters, digits and punctuation. The standard billing unit for translations and print shops.

items.title: Sentences
items.description: Number of sentences in the text. Helps assess complexity and readability — shorter sentences are easier to absorb.

items.title: Paragraphs
items.description: Text blocks separated by blank lines. Well-structured paragraphs improve readability on screens.

items.title: Unique words
items.description: Number of non-repeating words. A higher ratio of unique to total words indicates richer vocabulary.

items.title: Average word length
items.description: Average number of characters per word. An indicator of text complexity — technical texts have longer words than casual ones.

items.title: Reading time
items.description: Estimated time to read the text at 200 words per minute. An approximate value for typical text.

contentBlocks.title: 10 text formatting tools
contentBlocks.description: Below the text field you will find a toolbar with buttons for quick text transformation:

items.title: UPPERCASE
items.description: Converts all text to uppercase. Useful for headings, acronyms and highlighting text fragments.

items.title: lowercase
items.description: Converts all text to lowercase. Useful for normalizing text copied from different sources.

items.title: Sentence case
items.description: First letter of each sentence is capitalized, the rest lowercase. Standard format for continuous text.

items.title: Title Case
items.description: Each word starts with a capital letter. Ideal for titles, headings and proper names.

items.title: tOGGLE cASE
items.description: Swaps uppercase to lowercase and vice versa. Useful when text was typed with Caps Lock on.

items.title: Remove extra spaces
items.description: Reduces multiple spaces to one and trims spaces at the beginning and end of each line.

items.title: Remove empty lines
items.description: Removes empty and redundant lines from text. Useful for cleaning text copied from websites.

items.title: Remove duplicate lines
items.description: Keeps only unique lines, removing repetitions. Useful for cleaning lists and data.

items.title: Sort A→Z
items.description: Sorts text lines alphabetically in ascending order. Useful for organizing lists, keywords and data.

items.title: Sort Z→A
items.description: Sorts text lines alphabetically in descending order. Reverse sort order.

contentBlocks.title: Who is this word counter and text tools for?
contentBlocks.description: The tool is useful for anyone who works with text:

items.title: Copywriters and editors
items.description: Count words and characters, check platform limits, quickly change letter case and clean text before publishing.

items.title: Bloggers and content creators
items.description: Monitor post length, check reading time, remove duplicate lines and sort lists.

items.title: Store owners
items.description: Verify product descriptions against character limits on platforms (Amazon, eBay, Etsy).

items.title: SEO specialists
items.description: Analyze content length, count unique words and assess text complexity based on average word length.

items.title: Students and academics
items.description: Check word or character limits in essays and research papers.

items.title: Translators
items.description: Count characters without spaces for quotes (the standard billing unit in translation).

contentBlocks.title: Character limits on popular platforms
contentBlocks.html:
"\"

The character counter is useful when creating content for platforms with length restrictions:

Platform / ElementCharacter limitNotes
Google - meta title50–60 charactersLonger titles are truncated in search results.
Google - meta description150–160 charactersThe description visible below the link in search results.
Amazon - product title200 charactersShort, specific title with the most important keywords.
Amazon - bullet points500 characters eachUp to 5 bullet points describing key product features.
X (Twitter) - post280 charactersStandard limit for regular users.
LinkedIn - post3,000 charactersAfter ~210 characters a "see more" link appears.

contentBlocks.title: What makes this word and character counter special?

items.title: 8 text metrics
items.description: Words, characters with and without spaces, sentences, paragraphs, unique words, average word length and reading time — all in one panel.

items.title: 10 formatting tools
items.description: Letter case conversion, removing spaces, empty lines, duplicates and sorting — without installing additional software.

items.title: Local processing in the browser
items.description: Your text is never sent to any server. All analysis and transformation happens locally on your device.

items.title: Copy and clear text
items.description: Copy the modified text to your clipboard with one click or clear the field to start over.

items.title: Instant results
items.description: Statistics update in real time as you type or paste text. No waiting for processing.

items.title: No registration or limits
items.description: The tool is completely free. No registration, login or personal data required.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about the word and character counter

items.question: How does the reading time calculator work?
items.answer: The tool divides the word count by 200 — the average reading speed for typical text. Technical or demanding text (e.g., documentation, terms of service) will be read slower. A light lifestyle article — faster. The result is an approximate value that helps estimate how long a reader will spend with the text.
items.answerSchemaText: The tool assumes an average reading speed of 200 words per minute. This is an approximate value for typical text.

items.question: What is the difference between characters with and without spaces?
items.answer: Characters with spaces includes all characters in the text including spaces, tabs and newlines. Characters without spaces includes only letters, digits and punctuation. Platforms like Amazon or eBay have limits in characters with spaces. Translation agencies and print shops bill by characters without spaces.
items.answerSchemaText: Characters with spaces includes all characters, characters without spaces excludes spaces and tabs. The difference matters for platform limits and billing.

items.question: How does the letter case conversion work?
items.answer: The tool offers 5 conversion modes: UPPERCASE (all text to uppercase), lowercase (all to lowercase), Sentence case (first letter of each sentence capitalized), Title Case (first letter of each word capitalized) and tOGGLE cASE (swaps uppercase to lowercase and vice versa). Click the appropriate button on the toolbar below the text field.
items.answerSchemaText: The tool has 5 letter case conversion modes: uppercase, lowercase, sentence case, title case and toggle case.

items.question: What does the 'unique words' metric mean?
items.answer: Unique words is the count of non-repeating words in the text. If the word 'marketing' appears 5 times, it counts as 1 unique word. A high ratio of unique words to total words indicates richer vocabulary and less repetitive text.
items.answerSchemaText: Unique words is the count of non-repeating words. A high ratio to total words indicates richer vocabulary.

items.question: How does removing duplicate lines work?
items.answer: The function compares each line of text with the others and keeps only the first occurrence. Repeating lines are removed. Useful for cleaning keyword lists, email addresses, spreadsheet data and any collections where duplicates may appear.
items.answerSchemaText: The tool compares text lines and keeps only unique ones, removing repetitions.

items.question: Is my text safe?
items.answer: Yes. All analysis and text processing happens exclusively in your browser — your text is never sent to any server. The tool does not save or store pasted text. After closing the page, the text is gone permanently.
items.answerSchemaText: Yes. Text is processed locally in the browser and is never sent to a server.
